meaning: n.空想家,培育动物(或植物)的行家,爱好者
examples: Since water is much cheaper than wine, and many of the fancier brands aren't available in stores, most diners don't notice or care.
But since water is much cheaper than wine, and many of the fancier brands aren't available in stores, most diners don't notice or care.
Although many other big firms resisted the temptation to over-borrow, developers borrowed heavily and built bigger and fancier hotels as if the whole world were planning a holiday in Las Vegas.
Instead of investing in a fancier house, stuff your tax-advantaged retirement accounts--before the kids apply.
The late 1990s saw the commercialization of fancier needles that could burn a wider radius.
